content.text: Circuit Tracks organizes your music into Patterns, Sessions, and Projects. Understanding this hierarchy helps you build complete songs.
Press the Pattern button to enter pattern view. The pads show your 8 available patterns per session. Press a pattern to switch to it immediately.

items: Up to 32 steps for each of the 6 tracks
Pattern length (can be different per track)

content.text: To create longer sequences, chain patterns together. Hold Pattern and press multiple pattern pads in order. They'll play back sequentially.

content.title: Song Mode
content.text: Chain all 8 patterns in order for a basic song arrangement. Pattern chains are saved with your project.
A Session contains 8 patterns plus all track settings (patches, samples, mix). Think of a session as one 'song' or 'scene.'
To change sessions: Press Pattern, then Shift + Pattern to see the session overview. Select a new session.
Each project contains 8 sessions. That's 64 patterns total per project.
When you select a new pattern during playback, it will switch at the end of the current pattern. This ensures your transitions stay in time.
Hold Shift while selecting a pattern to switch immediately on the next beat.
